topics.title: George Takei's Career and Fatherhood
topics.summary: The show features George Takei discussing his role in the series Heroes, his experiences with improvisational comedy, and the challenges of navigating conversations about drugs and parenting with his children.

topics.title: Personal Anecdotes and Late Night Routines
topics.summary: The studio features a conversation regarding personal relationships, the health of a colleague named Artie, and a detailed recount of a host's nightly routine involving chess, sleep aids, and adult media.

topics.title: Speculation on Artie's Health and Substance Use
topics.summary: The studio discusses the possibility of a colleague's health issues versus potential drug use, debating whether to request a hair follicle test for confirmation.

topics.title: Guest Appearances and Show News
topics.summary: The show features a guest appearance by George Takei, a discussion regarding a caller's experience on Deal or No Deal, an update from the newsroom, and an announcement for a countdown of Howard Stern songs.

topics.title: Cousin Brucie and Music Performance Reviews
topics.summary: The studio discusses Cousin Brucie's upcoming radio appearance and reviews a list of poor musical performances compiled by an engineer.

topics.title: Satellite Radio Merger and Political Commentary
topics.summary: The studio discusses the upcoming merger of Sirius and XM satellite radio, potential government censorship, a report on former Attorney General John Ashcroft's involvement in lobbying, and media coverage regarding Rudy Giuliani and Tyra Banks.

topics.title: Tyra Banks and Body Image Advocacy
topics.summary: The show features a discussion regarding Tyra Banks' public stance on body positivity, her history as the first African-American woman on the Sports Illustrated swimsuit cover, and an interview with a magazine editor about the public response to her weight disclosure.

topics.title: Black History Trivia and Contest Disputes
topics.summary: The studio engages in a trivia session regarding Black history before transitioning into a heated debate over a contestant's breach of confidentiality and the logistics of an upcoming weight loss competition.

topics.title: Confrontation Over Past Betrayals
topics.summary: The studio hosts a tense confrontation between two individuals regarding past professional betrayals, personal drug use, and the promotion of an energy product.

topics.title: Military Service and Personal History
topics.summary: The studio features a heated debate regarding military service claims, personal trauma, and the authenticity of reported medical conditions.

topics.title: Military Service and Mental Health Discussion
topics.summary: The studio explores a guest's military experiences, his mental health struggles, and the complexities of trust within the radio industry.

topics.title: Star Trek Trivia and Comedy Sketches
topics.summary: The studio features discussions on Star Trek lore, the mental health of a former colleague, and a comedic prank involving multiple callers and a Nissan Sentra.

topics.title: School Safety and Teacher Scandals
topics.summary: The studio discusses reports of a serious injury during a "cop check" in a high school locker room, followed by a discussion on a legal case involving a teacher's inappropriate relationship with a student.

topics.title: Media News and Military Aftercare
topics.summary: The studio discusses various topics including celebrity news regarding Anna Nicole Smith, the television landscape for late-night shows, upcoming changes to Daylight Savings Time, and reports of poor medical care for wounded veterans at Walter Reed Hospital.

topics.title: Political Commentary and Entertainment News
topics.summary: The studio discusses political figures including Ann Coulter, Hillary Clinton, Barack Obama, and Rudy Giuliani, alongside entertainment news regarding the NAACP Image Awards and recent box office performances.

transcript.text: Hey now, hey now, hey now, I guess who's not here today and guess who is here today?
I'm seeing somebody checking in and I see an empty chair.
Well, I see the star of Heroes.
Ah.
Good morning.
Oh, wait a second, I gotta put up George's mic.
Now, what are you doing?
I don't have George's mic on.
Hi, George.
How are you?
Good to see you.
Thank you for being here.
Congratulations.
Oh, that's right.
On two counts.
What are you counting?
Oh.
Two mergers.
Two potential mergers.
Two potential mergers.
Two potential mergers.
A personal one.
A personal one, that's right.
You know, I'm glad you're here, George.
I got a lot of people congratulating me about my merger with Beth and I got this very beautiful
bouquet of flowers from Melissa Etheridge and her wife.
Really?
That's right.
I love them.
But the card kind of struck me as odd.
What did it say?
I know them.
I met them at a function and they're very nice.
I like them.
Very nice people.
Had a nice night.
They were, you know, I met them for a few minutes.
Oh.
Congratulations on your engagement.
We won't recognize your marriage, but we hope you'll have a wonderful life together.
Love Melissa and Tammy Etheridge.
Oh, I understand that.
But I feel that's not a very positive...
No, no, no, they're saying that...
Gay marriage.
Yeah.
Right.
But what does that mean?
We won't recognize your...
I recognize their marriage.
But, you know, they're saying that they would love to recognize your marriage.
Like you would love to recognize theirs.
But I do.
But society so far has stood in the way of their union being recognized.
That's exactly it.
But why not say that?
Well, I mean, you didn't want to write forever.
They're putting it in a roundabout way.
Yeah.
But, you know, I also recall Howard, you're saying that you will not get married until
it's legal and equal for gays and lesbians.
And I tried that.
He forgot.
You forgot.
Well, love overcomes even those vows.
That's right.
I was willing to wait till gays had full rights, but Beth wasn't, I don't think.
She said look.
I'm certainly delighted for you.
Thank you.
I think it's wonderful.
Thank you.
And I just want, you know...
What about you, Mary?
What about a double ceremony?
What about a double ceremony?
You and Brad?
Me and Beth?
Well, you know what?
Today, Brad and I are celebrating our 20th anniversary.
Wow.
So you're already married.
Well, in our minds and hearts we are.
Yeah.
Hey, by the way, that's such a great role on Heroes, the last episode.
Fabulous.
I'm shocked at you.
I didn't realize...
Do you understand what I'm saying?
I mean, it's really an incredible commentary on American television audience now, you know,
on prime time network television, whole scenes are being played out in Japanese with English
subtitles.
That was only, you know, a few years ago that it was Art House Fair.
Right.
Will you ever get to speak English on the show?
Yeah.
Well, did you see last week?
Yes.
Yeah.
Were you speaking English?
HRG speaks Japanese.
Oh, right.
Right, right.
That's right.
It was the funniest thing.
But you don't speak English.
He speaks Japanese with you.
He speaks Japanese and he's supposed to think in Japanese.
Yes, he does.
But it was all phonetically memorized.
And when he came on the set, he said, "I'm the deer.
There's the headlight."
What?
Oh.
All right.
Hey, you know what, do you realize you're in another series that has the potential to
have the kind of fan worship that Star Trek has?
It is a great feeling, but you could theoretically...
You could theoretically end up at double the amount of conventions every year between...
Oh, it's a hero crew.
Well, you know, as a matter of fact, next month I'm doing a convention in Burbank that's
a Star Trek heroes convention.
Oh, my goodness.
Say who?
Are you getting paid twice?
And they're getting paid twice.
Are you getting paid twice?
Alas.
No.
No.
Just one.
Not yet.
The one convention.
Who shows up from the heroes convention?
It's a... panateer, Hayden Panateer, the cheerleader.
Right.
And Isaac.
Do all the geeks think they can get laid by hanging out with Hayden Panateer?
Everybody wants a car, right?
How old is she?
She's, I think, 18 or 19.
Yeah, probably everyone's trying to get in her pants, huh?
She's very young and very sweet and quite an independent young lady.
I mean, she can hold her own.
Was she boning?
Is she doing it yet?
What?
Did she come home with anyone?
I don't know, you know, because it's an ensemble cast, you know, I'm not there with them all
the time.
It's just like, you know, actually, it's they've all been one dares, you know, when I'm on.
It's that Japanese sequence.
So you know, I do it in one day.
You know, I became friendly with the guy who plays the politician, Adrian.
Oh, Adrian.
Yeah, Pastor.
Yeah, I met him on a vacation and we were hanging out a bit.
When you meet somebody, you become friends?

Yeah.
Well, he's at the chess.
So I turned him on to the ICC and all that.
So are you playing him?
Is he good?
I don't know.
He no, he's not.
I mean, I don't know if he's good or bad.
He just kind of like is never on there to beat the guy.
What's he like?
He's a he's a real nice guy.
Real nice guy.
And he was just in New York.
He invited me to dinner, but I was busy that night using for one day.
By the way, I wanted to ask you the whole time this series has been on the cheerleader's

Do you know who he is?
Have you seen him before?
Jack Coleman.
He looks familiar that you mentioned it.
What has he been on?
He was on Dynasty.
He was the gay character on Dynasty.
Oh, yeah.
Yeah.
He was this like the son.
Blake's son.
Boy, he got old.
Yeah.
He got, you know.
He's a real nice guy.
I'm sure he is.
As a matter of fact, I...
Is he gay in real life or is he straight?
He's straight.
He's married.
He's married kids.
Oh, okay.

I took my nephews, two little ones who are the apples of my eyes, and there was Jack.
And so we traded the hopes and expectations about Heroes, but he brought his little kid
over and...
Looks to me like they've worked in as a series regular because now is it...
I thought you were going to be a one-shot deal just playing Heroes' father.
That's what I thought, too.
And now all of a sudden the plot thickens.
You're a major portion of the plot.
I am very intertwined.
Yeah.
Thank God for intertwined.
To be there.
But you know, the thing is they don't want actors to know too much about what's going

Believe me, they don't know what's going to happen.
They don't know what's going to happen either.
And so, you know, I'm making discoveries with each new script.
I'm like, "Oh, wow."

Like what episode are you up to?
Uh, the last week was 17.
Right.
Chapter 17.
And the next one coming up for me is 19.
Oh, so they shoot them pretty quick and end them fast.
Pretty fast, yeah.
Jeez, how do they do that?
But that's a big chance.
And then they take long hiatus.
Yeah, well, they're going to be off for six weeks.
And you know what?

And it's the funniest thing.
We don't know.
You know, they get celebrities.
And we show up and they put us into a costume.

You have to jump in.
It's an improv.
Is it funny?
It is.
I mean, it's terrifying.
You know, you're boldly going where you've never been before.
You know, you don't know what's happening.

And he was on a Star Trek type set.
And he had to wink it off.
Well, he would have loved that.
He was a big fan of Star Trek.

here?
Like maybe-- That's exactly it.
Do you attribute any of this radio show to some of the success?
Howard.
You are amazing.
Wow.

Wow.

Great.
Great.
Great.
Great.
Great.
So you're the key.
Good.
Oh, wow.
Well, my life has opened up.
Now, aren't you a little bit sad that you waited so long?
We weren't asking you for years.
I was begging you for Christ's sake.
I told you to come on here.
Well, you know, when I first met you, I didn't-- this was like doing Thank God You're Here.
I was in a play here.
I had, you know, your assignment when you're doing the play, and they gave me this address.
You were on Madison Avenue then.
I went to that address.
I sat and waited while the radio show was playing.
And I said to this guy that was waiting there, you know, why don't they get some nice music

This is vulgar.
You were a lot more uptight back then.
And they dropped me into your lap.
I was--
You know what's weird?
Fred came to me the other day, and he said, I can't believe George is getting all this

I've been on the show 20-something years.
I know.
It hasn't affected his career at all.
Yeah.

Nobody's saying Thank God I'm here.
Right.
But I am thrilled for you.
And I love your character on Heroes.
I think it's a character that shows a lot of promise.

see the role getting bigger and bigger, and I'd be very happy for you.
Stay tuned.

Prescient.
Well, I know.
You're good.

You know what I mean?
I'm having a great time there.
Yeah, good for you.
And--

The usual stuff.
Yeah.

So you have to be selective.

Oh.

Right.
And--

Yes.
And this show, Howard, you have been--
Well, the fertilizer--

Good to be back aboard.

Well, I don't know.
What's happening?
Artie called in sick this morning again.
Oh, I'm sorry to hear that.

You know, I do worry when Artie's not here.
Anytime.
Well, there's a vacancy here.

My favorite breakfast--
My muffin returns.

But--
Oh.

Oh.

Okay.
